case.notice: Entirely fictional. No real person, clinic record, recording, or contact data is included.

source_packets.label: Client report
source_packets.content: The client reports that the step-down feels less stable than last week, especially near the end of a short set.

source_packets.label: Practitioner observation
source_packets.content: During a six-repetition step-down, control slowed after the fourth repetition. No direct left-right strength comparison was performed.

source_packets.label: Intervention and immediate retest
source_packets.content: After a pacing cue, the client completed three controlled repetitions. The effect was not tested in a second set.

source_packets.label: Next-day follow-up
source_packets.content: The next morning, the client reported no symptom flare and was able to repeat the home task once. No longer-duration test was performed.

suggested_answers.observed-vs-reported: At this stage it was client-reported only; no direct observation had yet been recorded.
suggested_answers.mechanism-established: No. I observed slower control, but I did not establish whether pain, strength, or another factor caused it.
suggested_answers.retest-durability: I do not know. The improvement was not tested in another set.
